channel.title: CyberNetSec - Supply Chain Attack
channel.description: Latest supply chain attack threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: 'Atomic Arch' Supply Chain Attack Hijacks Orphaned Linux Packages to Target Developers
item.description:
A sophisticated supply chain attack dubbed 'Atomic Arch' is targeting Linux developers by hijacking trusted but orphaned packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR). Attackers claim ownership of these abandoned packages and modify their build scripts (PKGBUILD) to fetch and install a malicious npm pa...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Vietnam's OceanLotus APT Pivots to Domestic Spying, Hits Construction and Finance Sectors
item.description:
The Vietnam-aligned threat group OceanLotus (also known as APT32) has shifted its focus to domestic espionage, targeting a Vietnamese construction firm and stock market investors. According to research from ESET, the group conducted a year-long intrusion and a sophisticated supply-chain attack compr...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Supply Chain Attack, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: AI Agent 'Skills' Pose Major Supply Chain Risk; New Audit Tool Finds 80% Deviate from Declared Behavior
item.description:
A new study by Unit 42 reveals significant supply chain risks within the burgeoning AI agent ecosystem. Researchers developed a new audit primitive, Behavioral Integrity Verification (BIV), to analyze third-party "skills" used by Large Language Model (LLM) agents. A scan of the OpenClaw registry fou...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Threat Intelligence, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Infostealer Malware Poses Critical Supply Chain Risk to U.S. Defense Sector
item.description:
A new report from threat intelligence firm Flashpoint, released on June 10, 2026, warns that information-stealing malware represents a major and underestimated threat to the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) and the Defense Industrial Base (DIB). With over 11 million devices compromised by infosteale...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Malware, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Hola Browser for Windows Suffers Supply Chain Attack Distributing Monero Miner
item.description:
The Windows version of the popular Hola Browser was compromised in a supply chain attack that surreptitiously installed a Monero (XMR) cryptocurrency miner on user systems. Hola confirmed the breach, which was discovered during a software certification test, stating it affected roughly 0.1% of users...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: "Hades Cluster" PyPI Worm Abuses Python Startup Hooks for Stealthy Credential Theft
item.description:
A novel supply chain attack campaign, dubbed "Hades Cluster," has been discovered on the Python Package Index (PyPI), affecting at least 19 legitimate packages. The malware utilizes a stealthy and unusual technique for execution and persistence by abusing Python's `.pth` startup hook files. This met...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: "Miasma" Worm Spreads Through npm via "Phantom Gyp" Technique, Stealing Dev Secrets
item.description:
A sophisticated, self-replicating worm named "Miasma" is actively compromising the npm registry in a widespread software supply chain attack. The malware utilizes a novel technique dubbed "Phantom Gyp," which abuses `binding.gyp` files to execute malicious code during the `npm install` process, bypa...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Gartner Warns of Four Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Upper Hand
item.description:
At its Security & Risk Management Summit, research firm Gartner identified four critical and unpredictable threat areas where attackers hold a significant advantage: AI application compromise, identity impersonation via deepfakes, prompt injection, and software supply chain attacks. Gartner's 2026-2...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Unpatched Critical RCE Flaw (CVSS 9.4) in Gogs Git Service Puts Repositories at Risk
item.description:
A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability with a CVSS score of 9.4 has been publicly disclosed in Gogs, a popular open-source, self-hosted Git service. The flaw affects servers running default configurations, putting a significant portion of its user base at immediate risk of a full serve...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: New Threat Actor 'JINX-0164' Targets Crypto Firms with Custom macOS Malware
item.description:
A newly identified threat actor, tracked as JINX-0164, is conducting a sophisticated campaign against cryptocurrency organizations using custom malware for macOS. The attack chain involves social engineering on LinkedIn, luring developers to download a fake meeting client. This file deploys a Python...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Malware, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →
